title: Ancipitia crocodiliceps
taxonomy: Ancipitia crocodiliceps is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of this plant includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Magnoliophyta, class as Magnoliopsida, order as Gentianales, and family as Rubiaceae. Additionally, the genus and species levels are classified as Ancipitia crocodiliceps. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other species.
uses: Ancipitia crocodiliceps has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. benefits: Ancipitia crocodiliceps, also known as the crocodile beard, is a plant species that is traditionally used in medicine.
